content_en:
A central contribution of Tabyin Kadhib al-Muftari is its preservation and organization of biographical information about al-Ash'ari. Ibn Asakir collected accounts from multiple sources — including some that were closer in time to al-Ash'ari than any surviving alternative source — and organized them to provide a coherent picture of al-Ash'ari's life, education, and scholarly development.

Ibn Asakir documents al-Ash'ari's Mu'tazili training under al-Jubb'ai, his break with al-Jubb'ai over the question of the best possible world (the story of the three brothers), and his public declaration of his new theological position. He preserves al-Ash'ari's own account of his journey from Mu'tazili rationalism to a position that he claimed aligned with Ahmad ibn Hanbal's traditionalism while using the argumentative tools of kalam.

The biography raises important questions about al-Ash'ari's final theological position and how his various works relate to it. Ibn Asakir engages with the question of whether Al-Ibanah — al-Ash'ari's most traditionalist-sounding work — represents his genuine mature position or an early phase. He presents evidence that al-Ash'ari maintained his commitment to the Hanbali creedal framework throughout his life while using kalam methods to defend it, interpreting his various works as consistent expressions of this single underlying orientation.

This interpretive framework — al-Ash'ari as fundamentally a Hanbali who used kalam defensively — was important for Ibn Asakir's apologetic purpose. If al-Ash'ari was genuinely aligned with the traditionalist creed, then the Ash'ari school's use of kalam methods was not an innovation departing from the Sunni tradition but a defensive deployment of rational tools in service of that tradition. The school's critics who identified kalam itself as the problem were therefore misidentifying the issue.

The biographical sections of Tabyin Kadhib al-Muftari preserve information available from no other source and remain valuable for historians of Islamic thought working on the formative period of the Ash'ari school. Ibn Asakir's access to documentation and living scholars from the tradition of transmission made him able to gather material that would otherwise have been lost.
content_id:
Kontribusi utama dari Tabyin Kadhib al-Muftari adalah pelestarian dan pengorganisasian informasi biografi tentang al-Asy'ari. Ibn Asakir mengumpulkan riwayat-riwayat dari berbagai sumber — termasuk beberapa yang lebih dekat secara waktu dengan al-Asy'ari daripada sumber alternatif mana pun yang masih ada — dan mengorganisasikannya untuk memberikan gambaran yang koheren tentang kehidupan, pendidikan, dan perkembangan ilmiah al-Asy'ari.

Ibn Asakir mendokumentasikan pelatihan Mu'tazili al-Asy'ari di bawah bimbingan al-Jubb'ai, perpisahannya dengan al-Jubb'ai atas pertanyaan tentang dunia terbaik yang mungkin (kisah tiga bersaudara), dan pernyataan publiknya tentang posisi teologis barunya. Ia melestarikan kisah al-Asy'ari sendiri tentang perjalanannya dari rasionalisme Mu'tazili menuju posisi yang ia klaim selaras dengan tradisionalisme Ahmad ibn Hanbal sambil menggunakan alat-alat argumentatif kalam.

Biografi tersebut mengangkat pertanyaan-pertanyaan penting tentang posisi teologis terakhir al-Asy'ari dan bagaimana berbagai karyanya berkaitan dengannya. Ibn Asakir terlibat dengan pertanyaan apakah Al-Ibanah — karya al-Asy'ari yang paling bercorak tradisionalis — mewakili posisi matangnya yang genuine atau hanya fase awal. Ia menyajikan bukti bahwa al-Asy'ari mempertahankan komitmennya terhadap kerangka aqidah Hanbali sepanjang hidupnya sambil menggunakan metode kalam untuk membelanya, menafsirkan berbagai karyanya sebagai ekspresi yang konsisten dari satu orientasi mendasar yang sama.

Kerangka interpretatif ini — al-Asy'ari sebagai seorang Hanbali pada dasarnya yang menggunakan kalam secara defensif — penting untuk tujuan apologetik Ibn Asakir. Jika al-Asy'ari benar-benar selaras dengan aqidah tradisionalis, maka penggunaan metode kalam oleh mazhab Asy'ari bukanlah inovasi yang menyimpang dari tradisi Sunni melainkan penerapan alat-alat rasional secara defensif untuk melayani tradisi tersebut. Para pengkritik mazhab yang mengidentifikasi kalam itu sendiri sebagai masalah dengan demikian telah salah mengidentifikasi isu tersebut.

Bagian-bagian biografi dari Tabyin Kadhib al-Muftari melestarikan informasi yang tidak tersedia dari sumber lain mana pun dan tetap berharga bagi para sejarawan pemikiran Islam yang mengerjakan periode formatif mazhab Asy'ari. Akses Ibn Asakir terhadap dokumentasi dan ulama-ulama yang masih hidup dari tradisi periwayatan memungkinkannya mengumpulkan materi yang kalau tidak demikian akan hilang.
content_ar: A المحوري contribution of Tabyin Kadhib al-Muftari is its preservation and organization of biographical information about al-الأشعري. Ibn Asakir collected accounts from multiple sources — بما في ذلك some that were closer in time to al-الأشعري than any surviving alternative source — and organized them to provide a coherent picture of al-الأشعري's الحياة, education, and العلمي development. Ibn Asakir documents al-الأشعري's Mu'tazili training under al-Jubb'ai, his break with al-Jubb'ai over the question of the best possible الدنيا (the story of the three brothers), and his public declaration of his new theological position. He preserves al-الأشعري's own account of his journey from Mu'tazili rationalism to a position that he claimed aligned with أحمد ابن حنبل's traditionalism بينما using the argumentative tools of علم الكلام. The biography raises المهم questions about al-الأشعري's final theological position and how his various works relate to it. Ibn Asakir engages with the question of whether Al-Ibanah — al-الأشعري's most traditionalist-sounding work — represents his genuine mature position or an early phase. He presents evidence that al-الأشعري maintained his commitment to the الحنبلي creedal framework throughout his الحياة بينما using علم الكلام methods to defend it, interpreting his various works as consistent expressions of this single underlying orientation. This interpretive framework — al-الأشعري as بشكل أساسي a الحنبلي who used علم الكلام defensively — was المهم for Ibn Asakir's apologetic purpose. إذا al-الأشعري was genuinely aligned with the traditionalist creed, then the الأشعري school's use of علم الكلام methods was not an البدعة departing from the Sunni التراث but a defensive deployment of rational tools in service of that التراث. The school's critics who identified علم الكلام itself as the problem were لذلك misidentifying the issue. The biographical sections of Tabyin Kadhib al-Muftari preserve information available from no other source and remain valuable for historians of Islamic thought working on the formative period of the الأشعري school. Ibn Asakir's access to documentation and living العلماء from the التراث of transmission made him able to gather material that would otherwise have been lost.
